source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer

finding.description: DYNAMIC trial randomized 455 stage II CRC patients to ctDNA-guided vs. standard management.
finding.effect_summary: 455 patients randomized to ctDNA-guided vs. standard management

source_excerpt: Of the 455 patients who underwent randomization, 302 were assigned to ctDNA-guided management and 153 to standard management.

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer

finding.description: ctDNA-guided arm reduced adjuvant chemotherapy from 28% to 15%.
finding.effect_summary: Reduced adjuvant chemotherapy from 28% to 15%

source_excerpt: A lower percentage of patients in the ctDNA-guided group than in the standard-management group received adjuvant chemotherapy (15% vs. 28%; relative risk, 1.82; 95% confidence interval [CI], 1.25 to 2.65).

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA analysis guiding adjuvant therapy in stage II colon cancer

finding.description: 2-year RFS non-inferior between ctDNA-guided vs. standard management: 93.5% vs 92.4% (HR 0.92).

source_excerpt: In the evaluation of 2-year recurrence-free survival, ctDNA-guided management was noninferior to standard management (93.5% and 92.4%, respectively; absolute difference, 1.1 percentage points; 95% CI, -4.1 to 6.2 [noninferiority margin, -8.5 percentage points]).

source.title: DYNAMIC 5-year follow-up

finding.description: 5-year RFS was 88% vs. 87% between ctDNA-guided vs. standard management.
finding.effect_summary: 5-year RFS 88% vs. 87%

source_excerpt: At a median follow-up of 59.7 months, 5-year RFS was 88% and 87% with ctDNA-guided and standard management, respectively (difference 1.1%, 95% confidence interval -5.8% to 8.0%), and 5-year overall survival is similar (93.8% versus 93.3%, hazard ratio (HR) 1.05; P = 0.887).

source.title: DYNAMIC 5-year follow-up

source_excerpt: 5-year overall survival is similar (93.8% versus 93.3%, hazard ratio (HR) 1.05; P = 0.887).

source.title: GALAXY/VEGA study

finding.description: GALAXY/VEGA enrolled over 2,000 patients.
finding.effect_summary: Over 2,000 patients enrolled

source_excerpt: "The GALAXY study, which is a part of the CIRCULATE-Japan project, is a large prospective, observational study that monitors ctDNA status for patients with clinical stage II to IV or recurrent CRC following curative-intent surgery. Of the 1,039 patients included in the ctDNA analysis, 18.0% (187 out of 1,039) were ctDNA positive 4 weeks after surgery, and 82.0% (852 out of 1,039) were ctDNA negative."

source.title: GALAXY/VEGA study

finding.description: ctDNA-positive patients receiving adjuvant chemotherapy had significantly improved DFS vs. observation (HR 0.39).
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA+ patients: adjuvant chemo vs. observation DFS HR 0.39

source_excerpt:
Looking through the paper for the specific hazard ratio of 0.39 for ctDNA-positive patients receiving adjuvant chemotherapy vs. observation...

After carefully reviewing all sections discussing ctDNA-positive patients and adjuvant chemotherapy outcomes, I cannot find any mention of a hazard ratio of 0.39. The paper reports different hazard ratios for ctDNA-positive patients receiving ACT vs. observation, but none match the claimed HR of 0.39.

NO_MATCH

source.title: GALAXY/VEGA study

finding.description: ctDNA-positive patients had DFS HR 11.99 vs. ctDNA-negative patients.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA+ vs. ctDNA- DFS HR 11.99

source_excerpt: In multivariate analysis for DFS in patients with pathological stage II–III disease, ctDNA positivity 4 weeks after surgery was the most significant prognostic factor associated with increased risk for recurrence (HR 10.82, 95% CI 7.07–16.6, P < 0.001).

source.title: GALAXY/VEGA study

finding.description: ctDNA-positive patients had OS HR 9.68 vs. ctDNA-negative patients.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA+ vs. ctDNA- OS HR 9.68

source_excerpt: postsurgical ctDNA positivity was associated with higher recurrence risk (hazard ratio (HR) 10.0, P < 0.0001) and was the most significant prognostic factor associated with recurrence risk in patients with stage II or III CRC (HR 10.82, P < 0.001).

source.title: ALTAIR trial

finding.description: ALTAIR trial showed FTD/TPI vs. placebo in Signatera-positive stage I-IV CRC yielded median DFS 9.23 vs. 5.55 months (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.55-0.98; P=0.0406).
finding.effect_summary: Median DFS 9.23 vs. 5.55 months, HR 0.75, P=0.0406

source.title: CITCCA study

finding.description: CITCCA study of 377 patients showed post-treatment ctDNA positivity carried HR 38.5 for recurrence.
finding.effect_summary: Post-treatment ctDNA+ HR 38.5 for recurrence

source.title: Guardant Reveal stage III CRC study

finding.description: Guardant Reveal stage III study showed ctDNA+ patients had TTR HR 5.96.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA+ patients TTR HR 5.96

source.title: Guardant Reveal stage III CRC study

finding.description: 5-year DFS was 27.7% vs. 77.1% for ctDNA+ vs. ctDNA- patients.
finding.effect_summary: 5-year DFS 27.7% vs. 77.1% (ctDNA+ vs. ctDNA-)

source.title: NCCN Colon Cancer Guidelines v1.2026

finding.description: NCCN recognizes ctDNA as a high-risk factor for recurrence in the adjuvant setting, Category 2A for stage II adjuvant therapy decisions.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA recognized as high-risk factor, Category 2A for stage II

source.title: ASCO Provisional Clinical Opinion 2024

finding.description: ASCO issued a Provisional Clinical Opinion endorsing ctDNA for stage II-III CRC to identify patients who may benefit from adjuvant therapy intensification or de-escalation.
finding.effect_summary: ASCO PCO endorses ctDNA for stage II-III adjuvant therapy decisions

source.title: COSMOS study

finding.description: COSMOS demonstrated 98.2% specificity for ctDNA detection.
finding.effect_summary: 98.2% specificity

source.title: Analysis of ctDNA to Predict Outcome after Surgery and Adjuvant Treatment in Colorectal Cancer

finding.description: Reinert et al. showed median 8.7 months lead time before radiographic recurrence.
finding.effect_summary: Median 8.7 months lead time before radiographic recurrence

source_excerpt: Serial ctDNA analyses revealed disease recurrence up to 16.5 months ahead of standard-of-care radiologic imaging (mean, 8.7 months; range, 0.8-16.5 months).

source.title: The role of ctDNA in precision adjuvant therapy for colorectal cancer: a scoping review.

finding.description: Postoperative ctDNA positivity strongly predicts recurrence and often precedes radiologic relapse by approximately 3-10 months
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA positivity predicts recurrence 3-10 months before imaging

source.title: The role of ctDNA in precision adjuvant therapy for colorectal cancer: a scoping review.

finding.description: ctDNA-guided management reduced chemotherapy use without compromising recurrence-free survival in stage II colon cancer
finding.effect_summary: Reduced chemotherapy use without compromising RFS in stage II

source.title: The role of ctDNA in precision adjuvant therapy for colorectal cancer: a scoping review.

finding.description: Escalation approaches including intensified chemotherapy or trifluridine/tipiracil have not shown definitive disease-free survival gains in ctDNA-positive patients
finding.effect_summary: No definitive DFS benefit from escalated therapy in ctDNA+ patients

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: Adjuvant nivolumab did not show statistically significant overall survival benefit in the intent-to-treat population for esophageal/GEJ cancer
finding.effect_summary: No significant OS benefit in intent-to-treat population

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: Overall survival benefit with adjuvant nivolumab appeared restricted to PD-L1-positive tumors in esophageal/GEJ cancer
finding.effect_summary: OS benefit restricted to PD-L1-positive tumors

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: Perioperative durvalumab plus FLOT demonstrated significant improvements in event-free survival and overall survival in gastric/GEJ cancer
finding.effect_summary: Significant improvements in EFS and OS

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: Adjuvant atezolizumab plus chemotherapy supported in stage III dMMR colon cancer
finding.effect_summary: Atezolizumab plus chemotherapy supported for dMMR stage III colon cancer

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: Encorafenib-based triplet therapy established as first-line standard for BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic colorectal cancer
finding.effect_summary: Encorafenib triplet as first-line standard for BRAF V600E-mutant mCRC

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: Trastuzumab deruxtecan confirmed as superior second-line therapy for HER2-positive gastric cancer
finding.effect_summary: Trastuzumab deruxtecan superior as second-line therapy for HER2+ gastric cancer

source.title: Practice-Changing Trials in Gastrointestinal Cancers at ASCO 2025: A Critical Review and Clinical Context.

finding.description: ctDNA-guided de-escalation reduces toxicity with outcomes approaching standard care
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA-guided de-escalation reduces toxicity with similar outcomes

source.title: Erratum: Tissue-Free Circulating Tumor DNA Assay and Patient Outcome in a Phase III Trial of FOLFOX-Based Adjuvant Chemotherapy (Alliance N0147).

finding.description: Erratum published for Alliance N0147 phase III trial evaluating tissue-free circulating tumor DNA assay and patient outcomes with FOLFOX-based adjuvant chemotherapy

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: 156 out of 328 screened patients with RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC were randomly assigned in a 2:1 ratio to cetuximab plus avelumab (104 patients) or cetuximab (52 patients)
finding.effect_summary: 156 patients randomized 2:1 to cetuximab+avelumab vs cetuximab

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Median progression-free survival in cetuximab plus avelumab arm versus cetuximab monotherapy arm
finding.effect_summary: mPFS 5.3 vs 4.3 months (HR 0.78, p=0.158)

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Median overall survival in cetuximab plus avelumab arm versus cetuximab monotherapy arm
finding.effect_summary: mOS 14.8 vs 12.9 months (HR 1.00, p=0.983)

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: 124 out of 156 patients had tumors without any genomic alteration in EGFR pathway genes (negative hyperselection)
finding.effect_summary: 124/156 patients had negative hyperselection profile

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Patients with negative hyperselection had significantly improved progression-free survival compared to positive hyperselection
finding.effect_summary: mPFS 5.35 vs 3.65 months for negative vs positive hyperselection (HR 0.62, p=0.017)

source.title: Cetuximab rechallenge in molecularly selected metastatic colorectal cancer: the randomized CAVE-2 GOIM trial.

finding.description: Patients with negative hyperselection had significantly improved overall survival compared to positive hyperselection
finding.effect_summary: mOS 15.0 vs 11.1 months for negative vs positive hyperselection (HR 0.61, p=0.037)

source.title: Translational feasibility of a plasmonic microarray-based liquid biopsy for KRAS codon mutation detection across tissue, plasma, and urine in early colorectal cancer.

finding.description: Plasmonic microarray achieved detection sensitivity down to 1 fM by direct hybridization
finding.effect_summary: Detection sensitivity of 1 fM by direct hybridization

finding.effect_summary: Detection sensitivity of 100 zM after blocked amplification

source.title: Translational feasibility of a plasmonic microarray-based liquid biopsy for KRAS codon mutation detection across tissue, plasma, and urine in early colorectal cancer.

finding.description: Clinical analysis showed 100% concordance between tissue, plasma, and urine in mutation-positive malignant cases when sufficient input was available
finding.effect_summary: 100% concordance between tissue, plasma, and urine in malignant cases
